perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE VII - BILLINGS AND PAYMENTS Transporter shall bill and shipper shall pay all rates and charges in accordance with Articles V and VI, respectively, of the General Terms and Conditions of the FERC Gas Tariff.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE VII - BILLINGS AND PAYMENTS Transporter shall bill and shipper shall pay all rates and charges in accordance with Articles V and VI, respectively, of the General Terms and Conditions of the FERC Gas Tariff. Payment shall be made within 30 days of the invoice date unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding payment terms. Article VII now states that payment should be in accordance with the FERC Gas Tariff but adds an additional condition: Payment shall be made within 30 days of the invoice date unless otherwise specified in a separate agreement. This contradicts Article VIII, which states that the agreement is subject to Transporter's Rate Schedule FT-A and to the General Terms and Conditions, creating uncertainty as to whether the 30-day clause supersedes, or is superseded by, the General Terms and Conditions regarding payment deadlines specified within that document, specifically if there are payment requirements in conflict with this new clause
perturbation.contradicted_text: ARTICLE VIII - GENERAL TERMS AND CONDITIONS This Agreement shall be subject to the effective provisions of Transporter's Rate Schedule FT-A and to the General Terms and Conditions incorporated therein, as the same may be changed or superseded from time to time in accordance with the rules and regulations of the FERC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a specific payment deadline (30 days) while Article VIII explicitly states that the agreement is subject to the General Terms and Conditions of the FERC Gas Tariff. This creates a contradiction because the FERC Gas Tariff might have different payment deadlines or conditions. The addition creates uncertainty and a potential conflict regarding which payment terms apply.

perturbation.original_text: 12.3 This Agreement will terminate automatically upon written notice from Transporter in the event Shipper fails to pay all of the amount of any bill for service rendered by Transporter hereunder in accord with the terms and conditions of Article VI of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.3 This Agreement will terminate automatically upon written notice from Transporter in the event Shipper fails to pay all of the amount of any bill for service rendered by Transporter hereunder in accord with the terms and conditions of Article VI of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff. However, the Shipper will have a grace period of 15 days from the date of the notice to rectify the payment before termination takes effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding termination conditions. Previously, section 12.3 stipulated automatic termination upon written notice if the Shipper failed to pay, in accord with Article VI of the General Terms, etc. However, now there is a condition being added, stipulating the Shipper will have a 15 day grace period from the notice, which creates an in-text contradiction. This makes enforcement unclear. This directly contradicts the automatic termination condition. The original terms of Article VI are now undermined by this new allowance of a 15-day grace period to rectify.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.3 This Agreement will terminate automatically upon written notice from Transporter in the event Shipper fails to pay all of the amount of any bill for service rendered by Transporter hereunder in accord with the terms and conditions of Article VI of the General Terms and Conditions of Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff. However, the Shipper will have a grace period of 15 days from the date of the notice to rectify the payment before termination takes effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates automatic termination upon written notice for failure to pay, aligning with Article VI. The changed text adds a 15-day grace period, directly contradicting the 'automatic termination' condition. This introduces a clear inconsistency regarding the termination process, which undermines the original terms of Article VI by allowing a grace period to rectify the payment, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted text is the same as the Changed Text and that contains the contradiction itself.

perturbation.original_text: 15.3 Unless otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, no modification of or supplement to the terms and provisions stated in this Agreement shall be or become effective until Shipper has submitted a request for change through PASSKEY and Shipper has been notified through PASSKEY of Transporter's agreement to such change.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.3 Unless otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, no modification of or supplement to the terms and provisions stated in this Agreement shall be or become effective until Shipper has submitted a request for change through PASSKEY and Shipper has been notified through PASSKEY of Transporter's agreement to such change. However, verbal agreements between the Shipper and Transporter shall be binding if both parties can provide records of the agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the modification terms in the agreement. Previously, changes were only valid if requested through PASSKEY and agreed by the Transporter via PASSKEY. However, now there is a condition being added, stipulating verbal agreements shall be binding if both parties have records, which creates an in-text contradiction. This is now uncertain, because it goes against the written/agreed via PASSKEY condition. This makes enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.3 Unless otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or Transporter's FERC Gas Tariff, no modification of or supplement to the terms and provisions stated in this Agreement shall be or become effective until Shipper has submitted a request for change through PASSKEY and Shipper has been notified through PASSKEY of Transporter's agreement to such change. However, verbal agreements between the Shipper and Transporter shall be binding if both parties can provide records of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by allowing verbal agreements to modify the contract terms, while the original text, which is also the contradicted text, stipulates that modifications must be submitted and agreed upon through PASSKEY. This creates a direct conflict, as now there are two conflicting methods for modifying the agreement, undermining the clarity and enforceability of the contract. It aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition where different sections provide conflicting obligations.
